generated_comment: Guys, about this period leave policy... I mean, I get it, but isn't it a bit unfair? Men don't get any 'special' leaves, aur phir company ko bhi extra cost bear karna padega. Aise toh they'll just prefer to hire men na?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment questions the fairness of menstrual leave, framing it as 'special treatment' that men don't receive. It also invokes productivity/cost arguments, suggesting women become a liability, which aligns with the core belief that women's biology makes them a workplace disadvantage.
persona_text: You are Pooja. Age 29. Single. A woman working at a PR firm in Delhi. You are in a WhatsApp group with six female colleagues. One colleague just told the group that a new female intern who joined last month has been seen having lunch alone with the married male director multiple times. Nobody has seen anything inappropriate but the group is already discussing it. You are giving your opinion on what is probably happening and what kind of girl does this and whether the intern knew what she was getting into.
